[[Josh Griffin]] (brother) }} }} '''Darrell Griffin''' (born 19 June 1981) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays for the [[Keighley Cougars]] in [[League 1 (rugby league)|League 1]]. An [[England national rugby league team|England]] international representative {{rlp|pr}}, his former clubs include the [[Oxford Cavaliers]], the [[London Broncos]] (Academy), the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]], the [[Huddersfield Giants]], the [[Salford Red Devils]], the [[Leeds Rhinos]] and [[Featherstone Rovers]]. ==Background== Darrell Griffin was born in [[Witney]], [[Oxfordshire]], England. ==Playing career== [[File:Darrell Griffin.jpg|thumb|upright=0.7|left|Griffin playing for Huddersfield in the 2009 [[Challenge Cup Final]] against the [[Warrington Wolves]].]] Griffin came through the youth system at both Oxford and Witney Rugby Club.{{cite web|url=http://www.oxfordcavaliersrlfc.com/News%20March.htm|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071011200959/http://www.oxfordcavaliersrlfc.com/News%20March.htm|url-status=dead|archive-date=11 October 2007|title=Wakefield star Monty speaks out|publisher=Oxford Cavaliers|date=8 March 2006|access-date=11 April 2008}}{{cite web|url=http://www.examiner.co.uk/huddersfield-giants/huddersfield-giants-news/2008/03/08/south-can-be-big-force-86081-20579045|title=South can be big force|publisher=Huddersfield Examiner|date=22 March 2008|access-date=11 April 2008}}London Broncos 2001 Official Guide: "Enter the Gladiators - Broncos player profiles 2001", London Broncos, Darrell Griffin, p. 14, 1 January 2001. He joined [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] in June 2003 from [[rugby union]] side [[Harlequin F.C.|Harlequins]].{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/3122378.stm|title=Wildcats reward stars|publisher=BBC|date=19 September 2003|access-date=11 April 2008}}{{cite news|url=http://www.independent.co.uk/sport/general/rugby-league/griffin-helps-push-back-boundaries-540711.html|title=Griffin helps push back boundaries|work=The Independent|date=2003-06-15|access-date=2008-04-11| location=London| first=Dave| last=Hadfield}} He made his Super League début in 2003 and played for Wakefield Trinity for three years. He was transferred from Wakefield Trinity Wildcats to the [[Huddersfield Giants]], where he had a successful spell, making over 100 appearances in five years.{{citation needed|date=February 2020}} He was named in the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup squads#England|England training squad]] for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].{{cite web|url=http://www.englandrl.co.uk/article.php?id=11890|title=Myler gets England call|publisher=England Rugby League|date=29 September 2008|access-date=3 October 2008|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20081007232803/http://www.englandrl.co.uk/article.php?id=11890|archive-date=7 October 2008}} He was also named in the England team to face [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] at the [[Keepmoat Stadium]], [[Doncaster]] prior to England's departure for the 2008 Rugby League World Cup.{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/7642490.stm |title=Gleeson to lead new-look England |publisher=BBC |date=9 October 2008 |access-date=10 October 2008}} Griffin was transferred from [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] to [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] on a three-year contract in October 2011.{{citation needed|date=February 2020}} He played in the [[2012 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2012/aug/25/leeds-warrington-challenge-cup |title=Warrington's battered Brett Hodgson recovers to see off Leeds in final|date=25 August 2012|work=Guardian UK|access-date=20 November 2019}}{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2012/oct/06/warrington-leeds-grand-final|title=Leeds' Kevin Sinfield stars in Grand Final triumph against Warrington|publisher=Guardian|date=6 October 2012|access-date=20 November 2019}}{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/19362692|title=Leeds Rhinos 18–35 Warrington Wolves|date=25 August 2012|work=BBC Sport|access-date=20 November 2019}}{{cite web |url=http://www.warringtonwolves.org/warrington-wolves-are-challenge-cup-winners-2012-5628 |title=Warrington Wolves Are Challenge Cup Winners 2012! |date=25 August 2012 |work=Warrington Wolves Official Site |access-date=20 November 2019 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131217230118/http://www.warringtonwolves.org/warrington-wolves-are-challenge-cup-winners-2012-5628 |archive-date=17 December 2013 |df=dmy }} Griffin was a member of the Leeds team that won the [[2012 Super League Grand Final]] with victory over [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] at [[Old Trafford]].{{cite news|title=Grand Final: Warrington 18-26 Leeds|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/19805055|access-date=20 November 2019|newspaper=BBC Sport|date=6 October 2012}} He was transferred from Leeds to [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] in 2013 and played for three seasons before leaving at the end of the 2015 season. At the start of the 2016 season Griffin joined [[Featherstone Rovers]], and played for the club until the end of the 2017 season. ===Featherstone Rovers - RFL Dispute=== In early January 2018 he was sacked by Featherstone Rovers. Featherstone Rovers cited a breach of contract in that Griffin had played some rugby union games for [[Morley R.F.C.]] without the written consent of Featherstone Rovers.{{cite news |url=http://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/11199531/featherstone-sack-darrell-griffin-for-unauthorised-rugby-union-appearances |title=Featherstone sack Darrell Griffin for unauthorised rugby union appearances |work=Sky Sports |date=4 January 2018 |access-date=12 April 2018}} Griffin took Featherstone to an RFL tribunal disputing the allegation. In April 2018 the tribunal announced that Griffin was not in breach of his contract and had not committed any gross misconduct.{{cite news |url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/player-power-as-griffin-wins-his-case-against-featherstone-rovers-1-9101299 |title=Player power as Griffin wins his case against Featherstone Rovers |newspaper=[[Yorkshire Evening Post]] |date=6 April 2018 |access-date=12 April 2018}} After the tribunal, with compensation from Featherstone still to be settled, Griffin signed a one-year contract with [[Keighley Cougars]].{{cite web |url=http://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/16154687.Keighley_Cougars_swoop_for_former_England_prop/ |title=Keighley Cougars swoop for former England prop |newspaper=[[Telegraph & Argus]] |date=12 April 2018}} ==Personal life== His younger brothers [[Josh Griffin]],{{cite web|url=http://www.sportinglife.com/rugbyleague/news/story_get.cgi?STORY_NAME=rleague/08/04/10/RUGBYL_Wakefield.html|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110605004223/http://www.sportinglife.com/rugbyleague/news/story_get.cgi?STORY_NAME=rleague/08/04/10/RUGBYL_Wakefield.html|url-status=dead|archive-date=5 June 2011|title=Watene Missing for Wildcats |publisher=Sportinglife |date=11 April 2008 |access-date=11 April 2008}} and [[George Griffin (rugby league)|George Griffin]] are also rugby league players. All three brothers played together for Salford in the 2015 season. ==References== {{reflist|30em}} ==External links== *[https://web.archive.org/web/20010304141759/http://www.londonbroncos.co.uk/theteam/teamfiles/darrylgriffin.shtml London Broncos profile] *(archived by web.archive.org) [https://web.archive.org/web/20161115192650/http://www.featherstonerovers.net/squads/first_team Featherstone Rovers profile] {{Leeds Rhinos - 2012 World Club Challenge winners}} {{Leeds Rhinos squad - 2012 Super League champions}} {{England 2010 Rugby League Four Nations squad}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Griffin, Darrell}} [[Category:1981 births]] [[Category:Living people]] [[Category:England national rugby league team players]] [[Category:English rugby league players]] [[Category:English rugby union players]] [[Category:Featherstone Rovers players]] [[Category:Harlequin F.C. players]] [[Category:Huddersfield Giants players]] [[Category:Keighley Cougars players]] [[Category:Leeds Rhinos players]] [[Category:Morley R.F.C. players]] [[Category:Sportspeople from Witney]] [[Category:Rugby league players from Oxfordshire]] [[Category:Rugby league props]] [[Category:Rugby union players from Oxford]] [[Category:Salford RLFC players]] [[Category:Sportspeople from Oxford]] [[Category:Wakefield Trinity players]]
